title: Peppery Malaysian Taleggio Satay
description: A shaggy, festive Malaysian dish featuring Taleggio cheese, inspired by the bold flavors of Southeast Asia. This flexitarian-friendly recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: Lime juice
ingredients.notes: Fresh or bottled, whichever is handy

instructions: Grill or broil the wood skewers for 2 minutes to prevent them from burning.
Cut the Taleggio cheese into 1-inch cubes.
In a blender or food processor, combine coconut milk, ginger, garlic, red pepper flakes, kecap manis, soy sauce, lime juice, fish sauce, and brown sugar.
Blend the mixture until smooth, scraping down the sides of the blender as needed.
Transfer the mixture to a saucepan and bring it to a simmer over medium heat.
Reduce the heat to low and let it cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
While the sauce is cooking, thread the cheese cubes onto the prepared skewers.
Brush the cheese with a little olive oil and season with black pepper to taste.
Grill or broil the satay for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until the cheese is melted and the skewers are golden brown.
Remove the satay from the heat and let it cool for a minute or two.
Serve the satay hot, garnished with chopped cilantro and a sprinkle of freshly ground peppercorns if desired.
Garnish with additional kecap manis or sriracha if you like a little extra heat.
Serve with a side of steamed vegetables or a simple salad for a well-rounded meal.
Have fun with it and get creative with your toppings!
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
